name: Oracle APEX Universal Theme Vita Reference Layer

referencePages.label: Reference Overview
referencePages.purpose: Index for Universal Theme tools, migration guides, CSS utilities, CSS variables, JavaScript events, JavaScript APIs, and change log.
referencePages.generationUse: Use as the implementation reference entrypoint when a prototype needs APEX-specific helper classes or runtime extension guidance.

referencePages.label: Migration Guide
referencePages.purpose: Guidance for refreshing Universal Theme, re-applying theme styles, restoring subscriptions, and migrating from other themes.
referencePages.generationUse: Preserve APEX theme boundaries and avoid inventing structures that would be hard to migrate or refresh in a real APEX app.

referencePages.label: Button Builder
referencePages.purpose: Builds APEX button markup and link attributes with appearance, Template Options, advanced settings, and icon picker choices.
referencePages.generationUse: Generate buttons with APEX button classes and icon semantics rather than generic framework button markup.

referencePages.label: Template Directives
referencePages.purpose: Documents conditional, case, loop, and token modifier directives for processing substitution strings and content.
referencePages.generationUse: When showing dynamic APEX templates, preserve directive syntax and distinguish template logic from visible UI copy.

referencePages.label: Color and Status Modifiers
referencePages.purpose: Documents color utility classes, stateful color classes, opacity classes, and shadow classes.
referencePages.generationUse: Use status utilities for semantic state emphasis; do not hard-code arbitrary one-off colors when APEX modifiers exist.

referencePages.label: Layout Modifiers
referencePages.purpose: Documents floats, vertical alignment, flex utilities, spacing, margins, padding, display, visibility, and responsive layout classes.
referencePages.generationUse: Prefer APEX utility classes for spacing, alignment, visibility, and responsive behavior in enterprise prototypes.

referencePages.label: Content Modifiers
referencePages.purpose: Documents text, heading, alignment, transform, style, weight, wrapping, and overflow content utility classes.
referencePages.generationUse: Use content utility classes to tune dense enterprise UI copy without introducing custom typography systems.

referencePages.label: CSS Variables
referencePages.purpose: Documents color variables, palette variables, stateful variables, component variables, shadow variables, and sample component CSS.
referencePages.generationUse: Bind custom prototype styles to APEX variables and local Vita tokens instead of duplicating disconnected values.

referencePages.label: JavaScript Events
referencePages.purpose: Documents Universal Theme events that implementation code can listen to and respond to.
referencePages.generationUse: Mention documented APEX events only for interaction behavior; do not invent non-APEX event contracts.

referencePages.label: JavaScript APIs
referencePages.purpose: Documents Universal Theme widget APIs available for runtime extension.
referencePages.generationUse: Use API names as implementation notes, not as visual decoration; keep generated prototypes compatible with APEX extension patterns.

referencePages.label: Change Log
referencePages.purpose: Documents Universal Theme version changes, improvements, accessibility updates, and bug fixes.
referencePages.generationUse: Treat APEX 26.1 as current evidence and avoid relying on deprecated theme behavior when generating new screens.

enterprisePrototypeRules: Use APEX utility classes and CSS variables before adding custom CSS.
Use Button Builder semantics for report links, static content buttons, toolbar buttons, and icon buttons.
Use color and status modifiers for semantic status states such as success, warning, danger, and info.
Use layout modifiers for spacing, display, responsive visibility, flex alignment, and vertical alignment.
Use content modifiers for headings, wrapping, text alignment, transforms, and dense enterprise copy.
Keep Template Directives visible only when the prototype is demonstrating APEX template logic; otherwise render the resulting UI state.
Treat JavaScript Events and APIs as implementation notes, not as visual affordances.
Target Universal Theme 26.1 behavior unless a prompt explicitly asks for legacy migration.
